meaning: vt.紧缩,节约,减少,删除,给...筑围墙vi.紧缩,节省
examples: Shortly afterwards, cuts in defence spending forced the aerospace industry to retrench.
Demand for credit is lower, as companies and consumers retrench.
They must retrench their expenditure for the purpose of making up the deficit.
Sharespost has had to retrench its own secondary business after the Facebook IPO.
Now the danger is that consumers will have to retrench, leading to a nasty economic slowdown.
